title: [$250] Make the Workspace/Domain member list multi-select, update `All members` to `Members`

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
We currently have a single-select list in the `Members` page of each Workspace and also for the `Members` page of each Domain. Changes required: - Update the list to multi-select - Rename `All members` to simply `Members` The driver is the upcoming release of more/custom admin roles, and the fact that it's already helpful to be able to select multiple role types within workspaces. We'll apply the same change to Domains, largely for consistency.

title: [$250] Android - Native crash (SIGSEGV) in JavaTurboModule event emitter callback

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
**Version Number:** 9.3.71-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Unknown **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **Issue reported by:** Internal **Bug source:** Sentry - https://expensify.sentry.io/issues/APP-8J5 **Device used:** Android (arm64-v8a), confirmed on Samsung SM-S948U, Android 16 **App Component:** HybridApp / TurboModules ## Description The app crashes with a native SIGSEGV signal on Android. The crash originates in the React Native TurboModule event emitter infrastructure, specifically when a callback fires on a background thread and attempts to insert into `eventEmitterMap_` after it may have been modified or freed. First seen: 2026-03-27 Last seen: 2026-05-13 Scale: ~4,500 occurrences, ~3,700 users affected ## Stacktrace SIGSEGV in: - `facebook::react::JavaTurboModule::configureEventEmitterCallback::lambda::operator()` - `std::__ndk1::unordered_map::operator[]` - `std::__ndk1::__hash_table::__emplace_unique_key_args` The crash fires from the `FileObserver` thread via a JNI callback chain: `facebook::jni → JCxxCallbackImpl::invoke → std::function → JavaTurboModule::configureEventEmitterCallback` ## Action Performed Unknown — no reliable repro steps. The crash appears intermittently and is not tied to a specific user action. ## Expected Result No crash. ## Actual Result App crashes with a native SIGSEGV signal on Android. ## Workaround None known. ## Platforms - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari

title: [$250] [Android] Receipt capture produces mostly black/blank image

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
**Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1778675274739809 ## Description When capturing a receipt on Android, the resulting image is sometimes mostly or entirely black/blank. This has been reported by multiple users and appears to be intermittent — taking a new photo (which creates a new expense) works as a workaround. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the Expensify app on Android 2. Create a new expense and use the camera to capture a receipt 3. Observe that the captured image is mostly or entirely black **Expected:** The captured receipt image should be clear and usable. **Actual:** The image is ~90% black/blank. Retaking the photo (creating a new expense) resolves it. ## Additional Context - Reported on Android devices only so far - There is speculation this may be related to a snapshot-based capture change on Android (though the related PR https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88575 has not been deployed yet, there may be other snapshot-related changes in play) - Multiple users have experienced this issue independently
